# Research: M2.1 — Coleta de Traces e Estratégias de Avaliação

**Generated by**: `/speckit-plan` | **Date**: 2026-07-09

---

## DeepEval-First Check (Constitution Principle II — MANDATORY)

> **Correction (constitution v1.1.0)**: This check was originally recorded as a "LangChain-First
> Check" against Principle II. Under the current constitution, `TraceExtractor` and
> `EvaluationStrategy` are evaluation-domain abstractions — they belong to Principle II
> (DeepEval-First), not Principle III (LangChain-First, scoped to bot-orchestration code). The
> conclusion below (custom implementation required) is unchanged; only the framing and the
> consulted source were corrected.

**Consulted**: DeepEval source/docs (`deepeval==4.0.7`, installed in `.venv`) — 2026-07-09

**Checks performed**:
1. Searched `deepeval` for a read-side trace-retrieval or trace-extraction abstraction
   (`deepeval/tracing/`, `deepeval/dataset/`, `deepeval/integrations/`).
2. Searched for a built-in "evaluation strategy by bot/app type" registry that maps an
   application category to a metric set.

**Findings**:
- DeepEval's tracing module (`deepeval.tracing`, `@observe`) is write-side — it instruments an
  app to *produce* traces/spans for evaluation. It has no role in reading pre-existing traces
  back out of an external observability platform (Langfuse).
- DeepEval ships `EvaluationDataset`, `Golden`, `LLMTestCase`/`ConversationalTestCase`, and the
  metrics catalog (`deepeval/metrics/*`), but no abstraction for "select a metric set given a
  bot's platform or type" — that mapping is inherently project-specific (Flowise vs.
  LangChain/LangGraph traces; RAG vs. Agent vs. Conversation bots).
- `langfuse.callback.CallbackHandler` (already wired in M1) handles *emitting* traces from
  LangChain/LangGraph bots; it has no role in the read/collect path either.

**Decision**: Custom implementation required under Principle II ("Only when DeepEval offers no
native equivalent MAY custom code be developed"). `TraceExtractor` and `EvaluationStrategy` are
this project's integration layer that adapts Flowise/LangChain trace shapes into DeepEval's
`TraceRecord`/test-case model — exactly the kind of project-specific glue Principle II
anticipates and permits.

---

## Decision 1 — TraceExtractor interface shape

**Decision**: `TraceExtractorBase.extract(records: list[TraceRecord], status: InteractionStatus | None) -> list[TraceRecord]`

**Rationale**: The extractor receives raw `TraceRecord` objects already fetched by `TraceRepository` and applies two concerns that differ per platform: (a) how to determine whether a record has a valid output (completed vs. interrupted), and (b) any platform-specific field normalization. Passing `status` to `extract()` keeps the status filter co-located with the platform knowledge that knows how to evaluate it.

**Alternatives considered**:
- `extract(record: TraceRecord) -> TraceRecord | None` — single-record interface discards batch knowledge needed for the hard-cap ordering. Rejected.
- Embedding status filtering in `TraceCollector` using generic `output is not None` check — breaks Principle I (single responsibility) and doesn't account for platform differences in output shape. Rejected.

---

## Decision 2 — TraceCollector collect() flow

**Decision**:
1. Validate `TraceFilter` (already enforced in `__init__`, so invariant holds here).
2. Read `platform` from `ConfigManager` via key `bots.<bot_id>.platform`.
3. Emit `DEBUG` log with extractor class and `bot_id`.
4. Fetch raw records via `TraceRepository.get_by_date_range(bot_id, start, end)`.
5. Delegate to selected extractor: `extractor.extract(records, filter.status)`.
6. Sort by `start_time` descending, take first 500; emit `WARNING` if truncated.
7. Return list.

**Rationale**: All I/O goes through `TraceRepository` (FR-002). ConfigManager is the sole config reader (Principle V). Extractor selection at collect()-time (not at construction) keeps the collector reusable across multiple bots per instance if needed in the future without breaking V1 contracts.

**Alternatives considered**:
- Inject extractor via constructor — forces caller to know platform, violating FR-004. Rejected.
- Extractor selection in a separate `ExtractorFactory` — adds indirection with no benefit in V1 where the factory would have exactly two entries. Deferred to V2 if the extractor count grows.

---

## Decision 3 — BotType YAML key structure in bots.yaml

**Decision**: Each bot entry in `bots.yaml` MUST have two sibling fields:
```yaml
bots:
  my_bot_id:
    bot_type: rag          # BotType enum value (lowercase)
    platform: flowise      # TraceExtractor selector
    # ... other fields
```

ConfigManager flattens YAML with dot-notation keys, so the collector reads:
- `bots.<bot_id>.bot_type` → coerce to `BotType`
- `bots.<bot_id>.platform` → `"flowise"` or `"langchain"`

**Rationale**: Keeps all bot metadata in one place, read via ConfigManager singleton. `bot_id` is passed at collect() call-time and used to look up config — never inferred from the ID string (FR-004).

---

## Decision 4 — Metric sets for V1 strategies

All metric names are canonical DeepEval metric name strings. `MetricFactory` (M3) will instantiate them.

> **Correction (2026-07-09)**: `ConversationStrategy` originally listed `conversation_relevancy`,
> which does not exist as a DeepEval metric. Verified against the installed `deepeval==4.0.7`
> metrics catalog (`deepeval/metrics/`) — the correct multi-turn relevancy metric is
> `TurnRelevancyMetric` (`turn_relevancy`). Corrected below.

| Strategy | Metrics (ordered) | Why |
|----------|-------------------|-----|
| `RAGStrategy` | `["answer_relevancy", "faithfulness", "contextual_precision", "contextual_recall", "contextual_relevancy"]` | Covers retrieval quality (precision/recall/relevancy) and generation fidelity (faithfulness, answer relevancy) per FR-006 |
| `AgentStrategy` | `["tool_correctness", "task_completion"]` | Covers tool selection accuracy and end-to-end task success per FR-007 |
| `ConversationStrategy` | `["conversation_completeness", "turn_relevancy"]` | Covers multi-turn coherence and per-turn relevancy per FR-008 |

**Rationale**: All three sets are non-overlapping (SC-003). Sets are intentionally minimal for V1; thresholds and LLM judge config are M3 concerns.

---

## Decision 5 — Hard-cap ordering guarantee

**Decision**: Sort by `TraceRecord.start_time` descending (most recent first), slice `[:500]`. Records without `start_time` (None) are sorted to the end.

**Rationale**: `TraceRepository.get_by_date_range` does not guarantee ordering from the Langfuse REST API. Explicit in-process sort is required to make the "most recent 500" guarantee deterministic regardless of API response order (SC-001, FR-001).

---

## Decision 6 — InteractionStatus enum placement

**Decision**: Define `class InteractionStatus(str, Enum)` with values `completed` and `interrupted` in `deepeval_platform/collection/trace_filter.py` alongside `TraceFilter`, since both are collection-layer concepts.

**Rationale**: `TraceFilter` is the only consumer of `InteractionStatus` in V1. Co-location avoids a standalone one-member module. If `EvaluationRepository` needs it in M3, it can import from the collection layer without circular deps (collection ↔ evaluation is one-way: evaluation depends on collection output, not vice versa).
